∀ᶠ (s : set α) in f.lift' set.powerset, ⇑μ s < ⊤
source
theorem measure_theory.measure.finite_at_filter.filter_sup {α : Type u_1} [measurable_space α] {μ : measure_theory.measure α} {f g : filter α} :
μ.finite_at_filter f → μ.finite_at_filter g → μ.finite_at_filter (f ⊔ g)
source
theorem measure_theory.measure.finite_at_nhds_within {α : Type u_1} [measurable_space α] [topological_space α] (μ : measure_theory.measure α) [measure_theory.locally_finite_measure μ] (x : α) (s : set α) :
μ.finite_at_filter (𝓝[s] x)
source
@[simp]
theorem measure_theory.measure.finite_at_principal {α : Type u_1} [measurable_space α] {μ : measure_theory.measure α} {s : set α} :
μ.finite_at_filter (𝓟 s) ↔ ⇑μ s < ⊤

Subtraction of measures #

source
@[instance]
def measure_theory.measure.has_sub {α : Type u_1} [measurable_space α] :
has_sub (measure_theory.measure α)

The measure μ - ν is defined to be the least measure τ such that μ ≤ τ + ν. It is the equivalent of (μ - ν) ⊔ 0 if μ and ν were signed measures. Compare with ennreal.has_sub. Specifically, note that if you have α = {1,2}, and μ {1} = 2, μ {2} = 0, and ν {2} = 2, ν {1} = 0, then (μ - ν) {1, 2} = 2. However, if μ ≤ ν, and ν univ ≠ ∞, then (μ - ν) + ν = μ.

Equations
  • measure_theory.measure.has_sub = {sub := λ (μ ν : measure_theory.measure α), Inf {τ : measure_theory.measure α | μ ≤ τ + ν}}
source
theorem measure_theory.measure.sub_def {α : Type u_1} [measurable_space α] {μ ν : measure_theory.measure α} :
μ - ν = Inf {d : measure_theory.measure α | μ ≤ d + ν}
source
theorem measure_theory.measure.sub_eq_zero_of_le {α : Type u_1} [measurable_space α] {μ ν : measure_theory.measure α} (h : μ ≤ ν) :
μ - ν = 0
source
theorem measure_theory.measure.sub_apply {α : Type u_1} [measurable_space α] {μ ν : measure_theory.measure α} {s : set α} [measure_theory.finite_measure ν] (h₁ : measurable_set s) (h₂ : ν ≤ μ) :
⇑(μ - ν) s = ⇑μ s - ⇑ν s

This application lemma only works in special circumstances. Given knowledge of when μ ≤ ν and ν ≤ μ, a more general application lemma can be written.

source
theorem measure_theory.measure.sub_add_cancel_of_le {α : Type u_1} [measurable_space α] {μ ν : measure_theory.measure α} [measure_theory.finite_measure ν] (h₁ : ν ≤ μ) :
μ - ν + ν = μ
source
theorem measure_theory.measure.sub_le {α : Type u_1} [measurable_space α] {μ ν : measure_theory.measure α} :
μ - ν ≤ μ
source
theorem measure_theory.measure.restrict_sub_eq_restrict_sub_restrict {α : Type u_1} [measurable_space α] {μ ν : measure_theory.measure α} {s : set α} (h_meas_s : measurable_set s) :
(μ - ν).restrict s = μ.restrict s - ν.restrict s
source
theorem measure_theory.measure.sub_apply_eq_zero_of_restrict_le_restrict {α : Type u_1} [measurable_space α] {μ ν : measure_theory.measure α} {s : set α} (h_le : μ.restrict s ≤ ν.restrict s) (h_meas_s : measurable_set s) :
⇑(μ - ν) s = 0
source
@[instance]
def measure_theory.measure.finite_measure_sub {α : Type u_1} [measurable_space α] {μ ν : measure_theory.measure α} [measure_theory.finite_measure μ] :
measure_theory.finite_measure (μ - ν)

Interactions of measurable equivalences and measures

source
theorem measurable_equiv.map_apply {α : Type u_1} {β : Type u_2} [measurable_space α] [measurable_space β] {μ : measure_theory.measure α} (f : α ≃ᵐ β) (s : set β) :
⇑(⇑(measure_theory.measure.map ⇑f) μ) s = ⇑μ (⇑f ⁻¹' s)

If we map a measure along a measurable equivalence, we can compute the measure on all sets (not just the measurable ones).

source
@[simp]
theorem measurable_equiv.map_symm_map {α : Type u_1} {β : Type u_2} [measurable_space α] [measurable_space β] {μ : measure_theory.measure α} (e : α ≃ᵐ β) :
⇑(measure_theory.measure.map ⇑(e.symm)) (⇑(measure_theory.measure.map ⇑e) μ) = μ
source
@[simp]
theorem measurable_equiv.map_map_symm {α : Type u_1} {β : Type u_2} [measurable_space α] [measurable_space β] {ν : measure_theory.measure β} (e : α ≃ᵐ β) :
⇑(measure_theory.measure.map ⇑e) (⇑(measure_theory.measure.map ⇑(e.symm)) ν) = ν
source
theorem measurable_equiv.map_measurable_equiv_injective {α : Type u_1} {β : Type u_2} [measurable_space α] [measurable_space β] (e : α ≃ᵐ β) :
function.injective ⇑(measure_theory.measure.map ⇑e)
source
theorem measurable_equiv.map_apply_eq_iff_map_symm_apply_eq {α : Type u_1} {β : Type u_2} [measurable_space α] [measurable_space β] {μ : measure_theory.measure α} {ν : measure_theory.measure β} (e : α ≃ᵐ β) :
⇑(measure_theory.measure.map ⇑e) μ = ν ↔ ⇑(measure_theory.measure.map ⇑(e.symm)) ν = μ
source
@[class]
structure measure_theory.measure.is_complete {α : Type u_1} {_x : measurable_space α} (μ : measure_theory.measure α) :
Prop
  • out' : ∀ (s : set α), ⇑μ s = 0 → measurable_set s

A measure is complete if every null set is also measurable. A null set is a subset of a measurable set with measure 0. Since every measure is defined as a special case of an outer measure, we can more simply state that a set s is null if μ s = 0.

Instances
  • completion.is_complete
source
theorem measure_theory.measure.is_complete_iff {α : Type u_1} {_x : measurable_space α} {μ : measure_theory.measure α} :
μ.is_complete ↔ ∀ (s : set α), ⇑μ s = 0 → measurable_set s
source
theorem measure_theory.measure.is_complete.out {α : Type u_1} {_x : measurable_space α} {μ : measure_theory.measure α} (h : μ.is_complete) (s : set α) :
⇑μ s = 0 → measurable_set s
source
def null_measurable_set {α : Type u_1} [measurable_space α] (μ : measure_theory.measure α) (s : set α) :
Prop

A set is null measurable if it is the union of a null set and a measurable set.

Equations
  • null_measurable_set μ s = ∃ (t z : set α), s = t ∪ z ∧ measurable_set t ∧ ⇑μ z = 0
source
theorem null_measurable_set_iff {α : Type u_1} [measurable_space α] {μ : measure_theory.measure α} {s : set α} :
null_measurable_set μ s ↔ ∃ (t : set α), t ⊆ s ∧ measurable_set t ∧ ⇑μ (s \ t) = 0
source
theorem null_measurable_set_measure_eq {α : Type u_1} [measurable_space α] {μ : measure_theory.measure α} {s t : set α} (st : t ⊆ s) (hz : ⇑μ (s \ t) = 0) :
⇑μ s = ⇑μ t
source
theorem measurable_set.null_measurable_set {α : Type u_1} [measurable_space α] {s : set α} (μ : measure_theory.measure α) (hs : measurable_set s) :
null_measurable_set μ s
source
theorem null_measurable_set_of_complete {α : Type u_1} [measurable_space α] {s : set α} (μ : measure_theory.measure α) [c : μ.is_complete] :
null_measurable_set μ s ↔ measurable_set s
source
theorem null_measurable_set.union_null {α : Type u_1} [measurable_space α] {μ : measure_theory.measure α} {s z : set α} (hs : null_measurable_set μ s) (hz : ⇑μ z = 0) :
null_measurable_set μ (s ∪ z)
source
theorem null_null_measurable_set {α : Type u_1} [measurable_space α] {μ : measure_theory.measure α} {z : set α} (hz : ⇑μ z = 0) :
null_measurable_set μ z
source
theorem null_measurable_set.Union_nat {α : Type u_1} [measurable_space α] {μ : measure_theory.measure α} {s : ℕ → set α} (hs : ∀ (i : ℕ), null_measurable_set μ (s i)) :
null_measurable_set μ (set.Union s)
source
theorem measurable_set.diff_null {α : Type u_1} [measurable_space α] {μ : measure_theory.measure α} {s z : set α} (hs : measurable_set s) (hz : ⇑μ z = 0) :
null_measurable_set μ (s \ z)
source
theorem null_measurable_set.diff_null {α : Type u_1} [measurable_space α] {μ : measure_theory.measure α} {s z : set α} (hs : null_measurable_set μ s) (hz : ⇑μ z = 0) :
null_measurable_set μ (s \ z)
source
theorem null_measurable_set.compl {α : Type u_1} [measurable_space α] {μ : measure_theory.measure α} {s : set α} (hs : null_measurable_set μ s) :
null_measurable_set μ sᶜ
source
theorem null_measurable_set_iff_ae {α : Type u_1} [measurable_space α] {μ : measure_theory.measure α} {s : set α} :
null_measurable_set μ s ↔ ∃ (t : set α), measurable_set t ∧ s =ᵐ[μ] t
source
theorem null_measurable_set_iff_sandwich {α : Type u_1} [measurable_space α] {μ : measure_theory.measure α} {s : set α} :
null_measurable_set μ s ↔ ∃ (t u : set α), measurable_set t ∧ measurable_set u ∧ t ⊆ s ∧ s ⊆ u ∧ ⇑μ (u \ t) = 0
source
theorem restrict_apply_of_null_measurable_set {α : Type u_1} [measurable_space α] {μ : measure_theory.measure α} {s t : set α} (ht : null_measurable_set (μ.restrict s) t) :
⇑(μ.restrict s) t = ⇑μ (t ∩ s)
source
def null_measurable {α : Type u_1} [measurable_space α] (μ : measure_theory.measure α) :
measurable_space α

The measurable space of all null measurable sets.

Equations
  • null_measurable μ = {measurable_set' := null_measurable_set μ, measurable_set_empty := _, measurable_set_compl := _, measurable_set_Union := _}
source
def completion {α : Type u_1} [measurable_space α] (μ : measure_theory.measure α) :
measure_theory.measure α

Given a measure we can complete it to a (complete) measure on all null measurable sets.

Equations
  • completion μ = {to_outer_measure := μ.to_outer_measure, m_Union := _, trimmed := _}
source
@[instance]
def completion.is_complete {α : Type u_1} [measurable_space α] (μ : measure_theory.measure α) :
(completion μ).is_complete
source
theorem measurable.ae_eq {α : Type u_1} {β : Type u_2} [measurable_space α] [measurable_space β] {μ : measure_theory.measure α} [hμ : μ.is_complete] {f g : α → β} (hf : measurable f) (hfg : f =ᵐ[μ] g) :
measurable g
source
theorem measure_theory.outer_measure.to_measure_zero {α : Type u_1} [measurable_space α] :
0.to_measure _ = 0
source
def measure_theory.measure.trim {α : Type u_1} {m m0 : measurable_space α} (μ : measure_theory.measure α) (hm : m ≤ m0) :
measure_theory.measure α

Restriction of a measure to a sub-sigma algebra. It is common to see a measure μ on a measurable space structure m0 as being also a measure on any m ≤ m0. Since measures in mathlib have to be trimmed to the measurable space, μ itself cannot be a measure on m, hence the definition of μ.trim hm.

This notion is related to outer_measure.trim, see the lemma to_outer_measure_trim_eq_trim_to_outer_measure.

Equations
  • μ.trim hm = μ.to_outer_measure.to_measure _
source
@[simp]
theorem measure_theory.trim_eq_self {α : Type u_1} [measurable_space α] {μ : measure_theory.measure α} :
μ.trim le_rfl = μ
source
theorem measure_theory.to_outer_measure_trim_eq_trim_to_outer_measure {α : Type u_1} {m m0 : measurable_space α} (μ : measure_theory.measure α) (hm : m ≤ m0) :
(μ.trim hm).to_outer_measure = μ.to_outer_measure.trim
source
@[simp]
theorem measure_theory.zero_trim {α : Type u_1} {m m0 : measurable_space α} (hm : m ≤ m0) :
0.trim hm = 0
source
theorem measure_theory.trim_measurable_set_eq {α : Type u_1} {m m0 : measurable_space α} {μ : measure_theory.measure α} {s : set α} (hm : m ≤ m0) (hs : measurable_set s) :
⇑(μ.trim hm) s = ⇑μ s
source
theorem measure_theory.le_trim {α : Type u_1} {m m0 : measurable_space α} {μ : measure_theory.measure α} {s : set α} (hm : m ≤ m0) :
⇑μ s ≤ ⇑(μ.trim hm) s
source
theorem measure_theory.measure_eq_zero_of_trim_eq_zero {α : Type u_1} {m m0 : measurable_space α} {μ : measure_theory.measure α} {s : set α} (hm : m ≤ m0) (h : ⇑(μ.trim hm) s = 0) :
⇑μ s = 0
source
theorem measure_theory.measure_trim_to_measurable_eq_zero {α : Type u_1} {m m0 : measurable_space α} {μ : measure_theory.measure α} {s : set α} {hm : m ≤ m0} (hs : ⇑(μ.trim hm) s = 0) :
⇑μ (measure_theory.to_measurable (μ.trim hm) s) = 0
source
theorem measure_theory.ae_eq_of_ae_eq_trim {α : Type u_1} {m m0 : measurable_space α} {μ : measure_theory.measure α} {E : Type u_2} {hm : m ≤ m0} {f₁ f₂ : α → E} (h12 : f₁ =ᵐ[μ.trim hm] f₂) :
f₁ =ᵐ[μ] f₂
source
theorem measure_theory.restrict_trim {α : Type u_1} {m m0 : measurable_space α} {s : set α} (hm : m ≤ m0) (μ : measure_theory.measure α) (hs : measurable_set s) :
(μ.trim hm).restrict s = (μ.restrict s).trim hm
source
@[instance]
def measure_theory.finite_measure_trim {α : Type u_1} {m m0 : measurable_space α} {μ : measure_theory.measure α} (hm : m ≤ m0) [measure_theory.finite_measure μ] :
measure_theory.finite_measure (μ.trim hm)

Almost everywhere measurable functions #

A function is almost everywhere measurable if it coincides almost everywhere with a measurable function. This property, called ae_measurable f μ, is defined in the file measure_space_def. We discuss several of its properties that are analogous to properties of measurable functions.

source
theorem subsingleton.ae_measurable {α : Type u_1} {β : Type u_2} [measurable_space α] [measurable_space β] {f : α → β} {μ : measure_theory.measure α} [subsingleton α] :
ae_measurable f μ
source
@[simp]
theorem ae_measurable_zero_measure {α : Type u_1} {β : Type u_2} [measurable_space α] [measurable_space β] {f : α → β} :
ae_measurable f 0
source
theorem ae_measurable_iff_measurable {α : Type u_1} {β : Type u_2} [measurable_space α] [measurable_space β] {f : α → β} {μ : measure_theory.measure α} [μ.is_complete] :
ae_measurable f μ ↔ measurable f
source
theorem ae_measurable.mono_measure {α : Type u_1} {β : Type u_2} [measurable_space α] [measurable_space β] {f : α → β} {μ ν : measure_theory.measure α} (h : ae_measurable f μ) (h' : ν ≤ μ) :
ae_measurable f ν
source
theorem ae_measurable.mono_set {α : Type u_1} {β : Type u_2} [measurable_space α] [measurable_space β] {f : α → β} {μ : measure_theory.measure α} {s t : set α} (h : s ⊆ t) (ht : ae_measurable f (μ.restrict t)) :
ae_measurable f (μ.restrict s)
source
theorem ae_measurable.mono' {α : Type u_1} {β : Type u_2} [measurable_space α] [measurable_space β] {f : α → β} {μ ν : measure_theory.measure α} (h : ae_measurable f μ) (h' : ν ≪ μ) :
ae_measurable f ν
source
theorem ae_measurable.ae_mem_imp_eq_mk {α : Type u_1} {β : Type u_2} [measurable_space α] [measurable_space β] {f : α → β} {μ : measure_theory.measure α} {s : set α} (h : ae_measurable f (μ.restrict s)) :
∀ᵐ (x : α) ∂μ, x ∈ s → f x = ae_measurable.mk f h x
source
theorem ae_measurable.ae_inf_principal_eq_mk {α : Type u_1} {β : Type u_2} [measurable_space α] [measurable_space β] {f : α → β} {μ : measure_theory.measure α} {s : set α} (h : ae_measurable f (μ.restrict s)) :
f =ᶠ[μ.ae ⊓ 𝓟 s] ae_measurable.mk f h
source
theorem ae_measurable.add_measure {α : Type u_1} {β : Type u_2} [measurable_space α] [measurable_space β] {μ ν : measure_theory.measure α} {f : α → β} (hμ : ae_measurable f μ) (hν : ae_measurable f ν) :
ae_measurable f (μ + ν)
source
theorem ae_measurable.smul_measure {α : Type u_1} {β : Type u_2} [measurable_space α] [measurable_space β] {f : α → β} {μ : measure_theory.measure α} (h : ae_measurable f μ) (c : ℝ≥0∞) :
ae_measurable f (c • μ)
source
theorem ae_measurable.comp_measurable {α : Type u_1} {β : Type u_2} {δ : Type u_4} [measurable_space α] [measurable_space β] {μ : measure_theory.measure α} [measurable_space δ] {f : α → δ} {g : δ → β} (hg : ae_measurable g (⇑(measure_theory.measure.map f) μ)) (hf : measurable f) :
ae_measurable (g ∘ f) μ
source
theorem ae_measurable.comp_measurable' {α : Type u_1} {β : Type u_2} [measurable_space α] [measurable_space β] {μ : measure_theory.measure α} {δ : Type u_3} [measurable_space δ] {ν : measure_theory.measure δ} {f : α → δ} {g : δ → β} (hg : ae_measurable g ν) (hf : measurable f) (h : ⇑(measure_theory.measure.map f) μ ≪ ν) :
ae_measurable (g ∘ f) μ
source
theorem ae_measurable.prod_mk {α : Type u_1} {β : Type u_2} [measurable_space α] [measurable_space β] {μ : measure_theory.measure α} {γ : Type u_3} [measurable_space γ] {f : α → β} {g : α → γ} (hf : ae_measurable f μ) (hg : ae_measurable g μ) :
ae_measurable (λ (x : α), (f x, g x)) μ
source
theorem ae_measurable.null_measurable_set {α : Type u_1} {β : Type u_2} [measurable_space α] [measurable_space β] {f : α → β} {μ : measure_theory.measure α} (h : ae_measurable f μ) {s : set β} (hs : measurable_set s) :
null_measurable_set μ (f ⁻¹' s)
source
@[simp]
theorem ae_measurable_add_measure_iff {α : Type u_1} {β : Type u_2} [measurable_space α] [measurable_space β] {f : α → β} {μ ν : measure_theory.measure α} :
ae_measurable f (μ + ν) ↔ ae_measurable f μ ∧ ae_measurable f ν
source
@[simp]
theorem ae_measurable_zero {α : Type u_1} {β : Type u_2} [measurable_space α] [measurable_space β] {μ : measure_theory.measure α} [has_zero β] :
ae_measurable (λ (a : α), 0) μ
source
@[simp]
theorem ae_measurable_one {α : Type u_1} {β : Type u_2} [measurable_space α] [measurable_space β] {μ : measure_theory.measure α} [has_one β] :
ae_measurable (λ (a : α), 1) μ
source
@[simp]
theorem ae_measurable_smul_measure_iff {α : Type u_1} {β : Type u_2} [measurable_space α] [measurable_space β] {f : α → β} {μ : measure_theory.measure α} {c : ℝ≥0∞} (hc : c ≠ 0) :
ae_measurable f (c • μ) ↔ ae_measurable f μ
source
theorem ae_measurable_of_ae_measurable_trim {β : Type u_2} [measurable_space β] {α : Type u_1} {m m0 : measurable_space α} {μ : measure_theory.measure α} (hm : m ≤ m0) {f : α → β} (hf : ae_measurable f (μ.trim hm)) :
ae_measurable f μ
source
theorem ae_measurable_restrict_of_measurable_subtype {α : Type u_1} {β : Type u_2} [measurable_space α] [measurable_space β] {f : α → β} {μ : measure_theory.measure α} {s : set α} (hs : measurable_set s) (hf : measurable (λ (x : ↥s), f ↑x)) :
ae_measurable f (μ.restrict s)
source
theorem is_compact.finite_measure_of_nhds_within {α : Type u_1} [topological_space α] [measurable_space α] {μ : measure_theory.measure α} {s : set α} (hs : is_compact s) :
(∀ (a : α), a ∈ s → μ.finite_at_filter (𝓝[s] a)) → ⇑μ s < ⊤
source
theorem is_compact.finite_measure {α : Type u_1} [topological_space α] [measurable_space α] {μ : measure_theory.measure α} {s : set α} [measure_theory.locally_finite_measure μ] (hs : is_compact s) :
⇑μ s < ⊤
source
theorem is_compact.measure_zero_of_nhds_within {α : Type u_1} [topological_space α] [measurable_space α] {μ : measure_theory.measure α} {s : set α} (hs : is_compact s) :
(∀ (a : α), a ∈ s → (∃ (t : set α) (H : t ∈ 𝓝[s] a), ⇑μ t = 0)) → ⇑μ s = 0
source
theorem metric.bounded.finite_measure {α : Type u_1} [metric_space α] [proper_space α] [measurable_space α] {μ : measure_theory.measure α} [measure_theory.locally_finite_measure μ] {s : set α} (hs : metric.bounded s) :
⇑μ s < ⊤
source
theorem piecewise_ae_eq_restrict {α : Type u_1} {β : Type u_2} [measurable_space α] {μ : measure_theory.measure α} {s : set α} {f g : α → β} (hs : measurable_set s) :
s.piecewise f g =ᵐ[μ.restrict s] f
source
theorem piecewise_ae_eq_restrict_compl {α : Type u_1} {β : Type u_2} [measurable_space α] {μ : measure_theory.measure α} {s : set α} {f g : α → β} (hs : measurable_set s) :
s.piecewise f g =ᵐ[μ.restrict sᶜ] g
source
theorem ae_measurable.restrict {α : Type u_1} {β : Type u_2} [measurable_space α] {μ : measure_theory.measure α} {f : α → β} [measurable_space β] (hfm : ae_measurable f μ) {s : set α} :
ae_measurable f (μ.restrict s)
source
theorem indicator_ae_eq_restrict {α : Type u_1} {β : Type u_2} [measurable_space α] {μ : measure_theory.measure α} {s : set α} {f : α → β} [has_zero β] (hs : measurable_set s) :
s.indicator f =ᵐ[μ.restrict s] f
source
theorem indicator_ae_eq_restrict_compl {α : Type u_1} {β : Type u_2} [measurable_space α] {μ : measure_theory.measure α} {s : set α} {f : α → β} [has_zero β] (hs : measurable_set s) :
s.indicator f =ᵐ[μ.restrict sᶜ] 0
source
theorem ae_measurable_indicator_iff {α : Type u_1} {β : Type u_2} [measurable_space α] {μ : measure_theory.measure α} {f : α → β} [has_zero β] [measurable_space β] {s : set α} (hs : measurable_set s) :
ae_measurable (s.indicator f) μ ↔ ae_measurable f (μ.restrict s)
source
theorem ae_measurable.indicator {α : Type u_1} {β : Type u_2} [measurable_space α] {μ : measure_theory.measure α} {f : α → β} [has_zero β] [measurable_space β] (hfm : ae_measurable f μ) {s : set α} (hs : measurable_set s) :
ae_measurable (s.indicator f) μ
source
theorem measurable_set.cond {α : Type u_1} [measurable_space α] {A B : set α} (hA : measurable_set A) (hB : measurable_set B) {i : bool} :
measurable_set (cond i A B)
